data.title: Tether: To date, it has invested $1.5 billion to expand commodity trade financing and explore a new model of stablecoins + physical assets.
data.description: Tether Holdings SA announced it has deployed approximately $1.5 billion in credit facilities to the commodity trade finance sector and plans to "significantly expand" this business. The company stated it will leverage its nearly $200 billion in asset reserves to provide loans in the form of US dollars and its stablecoin USDT, covering commodity trading such as oil, cotton, and wheat. This move is seen as a strategic shift for Tether from a single stablecoin issuer to a comprehensive "digital asset + trade finance" platform, potentially driving further integration of stablecoins with traditional physical assets. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: After making nearly $3 million in profit from swing trading of ETH, a major whale then repurchased nearly 20,000 ETH.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, a whale/institution that had profited $96.67 million through ETH trading sold 10,000 ETH at $3,597 on November 10, making a profit of $2.93 million. In the past day, it repurchased 19,945 ETH ($63.82 million) at an average price of $3,200 through Coinbase, Wintertermute, and FalconX.

data.title: Harvard University Increases Holdings in IBIT and GLD
data.description: According to PANews, as of September 30, Harvard University significantly increased its holdings in IBIT and GLD, as revealed by 13F filings. The university holds 6,813,612 shares of IBIT, valued at $442.9 million, and 661,391 shares of the GLD gold ETF, worth $235 million. This marks a substantial increase from the end of June, when Harvard held 1,906,000 shares of IBIT and 333,000 shares of GLD, reflecting growth rates of 257.48% and 98.62%, respectively. Additionally, Harvard owns 583,931 shares of Nvidia, valued at $109 million.

data.title: Multicoin co-founder: Large holdings have been built on ENA this year.
data.description: Multicoin co-founder Kyle Samani posted on X, "We've built a big position in ENA this year." This follows previous reports that Multicoin Capital's Liquid Fund announced an investment in Ethena Protocol's native token, ENA.

data.title: Canary Capital may suspend new ETF applications after launching XRP.
data.description: Canary Capital CEO Steve McClurg stated that the company has submitted applications for all currently eligible tokens under existing regulatory guidelines. The company may pause new cryptocurrency ETF applications for the remainder of the year, with the need for further documentation depending on regulatory changes or whether new assets meet the requirements of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The company will shift its focus to managing products such as its newly launched XRP ETF, which saw $58 million in trading volume on its first day. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Yala's stablecoin YU is experiencing suspicious activity similar to USDX, with related addresses lending USDC at extremely high interest rates but failing to repay.
data.description: DeFi community member YAM posted on X that they have observed warning signs for Yala's stablecoin YU. An address closely associated with Yala is borrowing the full amount of USDC and most of YU from the Yala Frontier market on Euler, and despite persistently high interest rates, has not made any repayments. The market's liquidity utilization has reached 100%, meaning lenders are unable to withdraw any liquidity. The Euler team has also set the lending limit for the Yala Frontier market to zero. Reportedly, the Yala team has not yet responded to inquiries from the Euler team or Discord community users. YAM added that YU is currently still pegged to the exchange rate on Solana, and its liquidity pool still has nearly one million USDC available for withdrawal at the pegged price. This post is primarily a risk warning, and it is still uncertain whether Yala is truly in trouble.

Shetty was reportedly hired in March 2021 as chief financial officer (CFO) of a […]

data.title: Over $1 billion in liquidations across the internet in the past 24 hours, primarily affecting long positions.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $1.08 billion in positions were liquidated across the network in the past 24 hours, including $909 million in long positions and $167 million in short positions.

data.title: Atlanta Fed President Bostic Weighs Future Rate Cuts Amid Economic Challenges
data.description: According to Odaily, Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic has indicated uncertainty about supporting another interest rate cut next month, despite backing the recent two reductions. Speaking at an event in Seattle on Friday, Bostic stated, "I was comfortable with the first two rate cuts, but as for the next one, we will have to wait and see. I want to rely on data to determine the most appropriate policy." Federal Reserve officials are currently divided on whether to continue cutting rates next month. This year, the Fed has reduced rates by a total of 50 basis points. Futures market pricing suggests that investors see a slightly less than 50% chance of another 25 basis point cut at the December 9-10 meeting. Bostic highlighted the challenging current situation, noting that "neither aspect of our dual mandate—employment and inflation—is moving towards the target." Earlier this week, he remarked that inflation remains a "more clear and pressing risk" in the economy.

data.title: Tether Explores Investment in German Robotics Startup Neura Robotics
data.description:
According to Odaily, Tether, the issuer of stablecoins, is considering leading a $1.16 billion funding round for German tech startup Neura Robotics. The two parties have initiated preliminary discussions, with Neura Robotics' valuation expected to range between $9.29 billion and $11.6 billion. Neura Robotics specializes in humanoid robot development, with its first product targeting industrial clients and plans to expand into the home robotics sector.

Tether has generated over $10 billion in profits during the first three quarters of this year and is seeking to diversify its investment portfolio.

data.title: Belarus President Lukashenko Supports Cryptocurrency Mining
data.description: According to Odaily, Belarus President Alexander Lukashenko has expressed support for cryptocurrency mining in the country. He believes that cryptocurrencies could potentially serve as an alternative to the U.S. dollar.

data.title: Coinbase launched its Business Account in Singapore, attracting over 100 institutional registrations in a short period.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong posted on the X platform, "Coinbase Business accounts have launched in Singapore. In just a few days, more than 100 companies and family offices have registered (some clients are still on the waiting list, and we are processing them as quickly as possible)."
